required_authority_family_coverage.label: Core NEPA statute, USDA/Forest Service NEPA procedures, EA/FONSI, CE/FANEC, tiering, and sponsor-prepared documents
NFMA, 36 CFR part 219, plan consistency, SCC, and Region 1 forest-plan overlays
Administrative review, final agency action, legal notice, objection/appeal, decision notice/FONSI, and APA final action
ESA Section 7, ESA regulations, BA/BE, consultation, concurrence, BO, and Forest Service sensitive-species policy
NHPA Section 106, 36 CFR part 800, SHPO/THPO, historic properties, adverse effects, mitigation, and cultural documentation
Clean Water Act Sections 401/402/404, WOTUS/wetlands, stormwater/NPDES, water-quality certification, Corps/EPA/state cues
Clean Air Act, smoke, prescribed fire, conformity, SIP, and air-quality permit/coordination cues
EO 11988 floodplain management and EO 11990 wetlands protection as separate authorities
Environmental justice and civil-rights authorities, including Title VI and current federal EJ requirements
Tribal consultation, trust responsibility, treaty rights, sacred sites, THPO, and sensitive cultural information
ARPA, NAGPRA, AIRFA, RFRA, paleontology, cave, and other cultural-resource protection authorities
MBTA, EO 13186, Bald and Golden Eagle Protection Act, EFH, and species/resource-specific wildlife obligations
Roadless, inventoried roadless areas, wilderness, WSR, National Trails, and designated/special areas
MUSYA, Organic Administration Act, roads/access, travel management, special uses, grasslands, minerals, and action authorities
Land-exchange-specific authorities, 36 CFR part 254, FLPMA/FLEFA, valuation/appraisal, notice, title, reservations, public-interest and equal-value requirements
Hazardous materials and site-condition due diligence, including CERCLA/RCRA/NCP and land acquisition/disposal triggers
Invasive species, noxious weeds, pesticide/herbicide, soils, watershed, riparian, fisheries, state/local permits, and common Region 1 project families
Litigation doctrine and review-risk authorities: hard look, alternatives, cumulative effects, segmentation, stale science, tiering, mitigation enforceability, and independent ESA/NHPA compliance

status_definitions.active: Source records, rule templates, trigger contracts, evidence filters, and eval coverage are implemented for the represented family slice.
status_definitions.candidate: The family is required or potentially relevant, but source records still need to be added, confirmed, or explicitly excluded.
status_definitions.out_of_scope: The family is intentionally excluded for Region 1 EA review with reviewer-visible rationale.
status_definitions.source_only: Source records exist, but rule templates, applicability predicates, coverage requirements, or eval cases are missing or incomplete.
status_definitions.superseded: The family or source has been replaced, reserved, repealed, or superseded; replacement/current-source evidence is recorded.
